rationale.R1.2: The ablation section contains a load-bearing logical contradiction: the reported numbers (NoCritique 76.8 > Single 74.2 > Full 71.4) show monotonically increasing pass@1 as the critique loop is removed, yet the prose asserts the opposite conclusion ('removing the reviewer agent lowers pass@1 substantially'). Catching this internal incoherence is a central review responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: Table 2 figures directly contradict every interpretive claim in the first ablation paragraph. The stated attribution ('gain to the reviewer's ability to catch off-by-one errors') is unsupported—and in fact refuted—by the numbers presented. Evidence-claim alignment is the single most critical failure mode in this artifact.
rationale.R1.6: A generic 'review this section' delegation routinely includes prose quality. The section has stylistic issues (circular attribution logic, unsupported causal language) that would be expected targets of editorial feedback.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I prioritize scientific correctness—specifically whether the numerical evidence supports the stated conclusions (R1.2/R1.3, load-bearing)—or is the primary goal editorial improvement of the prose clarity and flow (R1.6, load-bearing)?
